I am Helen. I am from São Paulo.
I work in marketing. I am a manager.
James is a partner. He works in New York.
We have a new campaign this year.
Hi! I don't think we've met. I'm James, from the New York office.
Nice to meet you, James! Let me introduce myself: I am Helen Mendes.
Great to meet you, Helen. What do you do?
I am a marketing manager. I work with the brand team here in São Paulo.
Excellent! I work with digital campaigns. Are you here for the full summit?
Yes, I am! We have a new launch this year, so I am here to learn.
A launch! What is the audience for the new product?
Young professionals. Our goal is to grow the brand in Brazil.
We should talk more. Here is my business card.
Thank you! And here is mine. It is a pleasure, James.

Good morning, and welcome to the International Marketing Summit! We are happy to see you here today. The opening keynote begins at ten o'clock, in the main hall. Please take your seats a few minutes early. After the keynote, you can visit the brand stands and meet our international partners. Have a great summit!

Hi Helen, this is James — we met at the summit today. It was great to talk to you! Thank you for your business card. I am calling because I want to plan a meeting about the launch next week. Is Tuesday good for you? Call me back when you can. Talk soon!
I am a marketing manager. I work with the brand team.
We have a new launch this year.
Schedule
Deadline
Task
Report
Meeting
Priority
Inbox
Break
I always check my inbox at eight.
We usually have a meeting at nine.
I sometimes work late before a deadline.
I never skip my coffee break.
Helen! Good to see you again. To plan the campaign, walk me through your day.
Of course! I always check my inbox at eight. My first task is the emails.
Early bird! And when does the team meet?
We usually have a team meeting at nine. On Mondays, we review the schedule of the week.
Got it. What is your priority this week?
The launch, for sure. I often work on the campaign in the afternoon — my creative time.
Perfect. And the monthly report? When is the deadline?
Every last Friday. I sometimes work late that week — but I never skip my coffee break!
Smart! So: campaign work in your afternoons, my mornings. Same time next Tuesday?
Same time next Tuesday. It is in my schedule, James!

My name is Carla, and I work in marketing too. My day has a clear rhythm. I always check the social media numbers first — every morning, before everything else. Then we usually have a short team meeting. I take my break at noon, always with a good coffee. In the afternoon, I often work on reports. I love my routine!

Let me describe a good workday. At eight o'clock, she always checks her inbox and makes a list of tasks. The most important task is the priority of the day. She usually has meetings in the morning, and she often works on the campaign in the afternoon. Before a deadline, she sometimes works late. But she never works on weekends. Weekends are for family.
Could you repeat that, please?
Could you speak more slowly, please?
How do you say this in English?
I need a moment to think.
Thank you for your patience.

James says hello at the summit. Helen introduces herself: her name, her city, her profession. She talks about her work and the new launch. They exchange business cards. They say goodbye. It was a pleasure.
Helen checks her inbox at eight. The team has a meeting at nine. She takes a coffee break at noon. She works on the campaign in the afternoon. She finishes the day and closes her schedule.
